title: Elliott Said to Take Almost 5% Stake in UK Distributor Bunzl
summary: Elliott Investment Management LP has taken an almost 5% stake in Bunzl Plc, people familiar with the matter said, after a profit warning last year sent the UK distributor’s shares tumbling.

summary: Singapore’s retrenchments climbed to their highest level in almost three years in the first quarter as firms continued to reorganize their businesses amid a more uncertain economic backdrop.

title: Lagarde Says Energy-Price Hikes Spreading Through Economy
summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde warned that high energy prices are starting to feed through to other parts of the economy.

title: Crop Prices Drop as Hormuz Reopening to Ease Farm-Input Shock
summary: Grain and vegetable oil futures declined on expectations that a potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz would improve access to vital crop inputs, easing the threat of food inflation from the months-long war in the Middle East.

title: India Trade Gap Shrinks, Sees Hurdles Ease With US-Iran Deal
summary: India’s trade deficit narrowed marginally in May, though it came in wider than forecast, as officials expressed hope that an interim US-Iran peace deal would improve the outlook.

title: US Futures Rise on Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz; SpaceX Gains
summary: US stock index futures climbed after Washington and Tehran reached an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices lower and easing concern about inflation ahead of Kevin Warsh’s first meeting as chairman of the Federal Reserve later this week. SpaceX shares rose in premarket trading, helping boost risk-on sentiment.

title: Traders Cut South Africa Rate-Hike Bets on Iran Peace Deal
summary: Traders have scaled back bets on additional South African interest-rate hikes after an interim US-Iran peace deal sent oil prices tumbling, potentially reducing inflationary pressures.

title: Braemar Hotels Holder Al Shams Accuses Firm of ‘Self-Dealing’
summary: Al Shams Investments Ltd. has criticized Braemar Hotels & Resorts Inc. over a series of recent transactions that it said amount to “a brazen act of self-dealing,” according to a letter reviewed by Bloomberg News.

title: Fed Decision Likely to Have No Dissenters, JPM’s Berro Says
summary: Kelsey Berro, fixed income portfolio manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, examines the bond market reaction to the announcement of an agreement between the US and Iran and looks ahead to this week’s Federal Reserve interest rate decision. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US and Iran Agree to Halt War, SpaceX Jumps | The Pulse 6/15/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Tina Fordham, Fordham Global Foresight Founder & Geopolitical Strategist; Karen Ward, JPMorgan Asset Management EMEA Chief Market Strategist; Maxim Timchenko, DTEK Group CEO (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Shipowners Seek Details on Hormuz Deal Before Resuming Transits
summary: A US-Iran deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within days has been met with caution by shipowners and traders, with many saying they would need more details in order to assess whether safe transits are possible after months of false starts.

title: Stocks and Bonds Rally as Oil Falls on Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks and bonds rallied while oil tumbled to a three-month low after the US and Iran agreed to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Treasuries Rally as Traders Trim Fed Hike Bets After Iran Deal
summary: Treasuries advanced across the curve as investors dialed back expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes following news of a deal to halt the Iran war.

title: Stocks and Bonds Rally on US-Iran Deal | The Opening Trade 6/15/2026
summary:
A global relief rally drove stocks and bonds higher while oil tumbled to a three-month low after the US and Iran agreed to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump said the waterway will reopen upon the signing of the deal on Friday. The event would then trigger the start of 60 days of talks on Iran’s nuclear program.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran, Fed Take Market Spotlight Away From SpaceX Frenzy
summary: Peace agreement spurs stock and bond rally, oil slump.

title: Danantara Mulls 30-Year Bond Days After Fund’s Debut Sale
summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is exploring a sale of 30-year bonds following strong demand for its first ever global issuance.

title: World Bank Plans $750 Million Delayed Loan for Kenya by End-June
summary: The World Bank plans to disburse a long-awaited $750 million loan to Kenya by the end of the month partly to help the East African economy ward off external shocks.

title: UBS’s Baweja Says AI Stumble Key to Longer European Stock Rally
summary: Europe needs to see several conditions in place, such as a broadening of capital expenditures away from artificial intelligence, before the region can sustain its equity rally, according to Bhanu Baweja, chief strategist at UBS Group AG.

title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Sees US Stock Market Rally Broadening
summary: US stocks could get an additional boost from a rotation into cyclical, economically-sensitive industries that have lagged in the Iran war, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

title: Zhipu Shares Surge 33% After JPMorgan Picks Company as AI Winner
summary: Shares of Chinese AI model maker Zhipu surged after JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised the stock’s price target and picked it as a winner against close rival MiniMax.

title: LBMA Says Gold Still Holds Safe Haven Status (Correct)
summary: London Bullion Market Association CEO Ruth Crowell thinks the narrative of gold's status as a safe haven still holds. She adds that it's important to measure safe haven status in years rather than weeks or months. She speaks with Paul Allen on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Corrects video to the right interview guest)  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: JPMorgan Sees Central Banks on Hold on US-Iran Agreement
summary: Karen Ward, EMEA chief market strategist at JPMorgan Asset Management, discusses the outlook for central bank policy after the US and Iran said they reached an interim agreement to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Ward speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Goldman-Backed Energy Trader to Raise Debt After 96% Profit Drop
summary: Danish energy trader InCommodities A/S plans to increase borrowing to support growth after profit slumped 96% last year amid subdued volatility in power and gas markets.

title: Birkenstock Readies First Bond Deal in Five Years for Buybacks
summary: Birkenstock is lining up its first bond offering in more than five years as it seeks to replace existing debt and considers further share buybacks.

title: Aluminum Slumps as Iran Peace Deal Softens Blow of Supply Crisis
summary: Aluminum slumped to a two-month low as a peace deal between the US and Iran laid the groundwork for the resumption of metal shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: K-Content Powerhouse JoongAng Seeks Court-led Restructuring
summary: The holding company of South Korea’s JoongAng Group, a sprawling media conglomerate and K-Content powerhouse behind hits like Netflix smash Culinary Class Wars, filed for court-led restructuring in Seoul.

title: Shell Is Said to Select Aditya Birla for Sprng Energy Deal
summary: Aditya Birla Group has emerged as the frontrunner to buy Shell Plc’s Indian renewable power producer, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Here Are Winners and Losers from Pakistan’s $68 Billion Budget
summary: Pakistan’s 18.8 trillion-rupee ($67.6 billion) budget, which includes lower taxes on sectors like cement and textiles, has lifted related stocks as the country seeks to balance growth with its International Monetary Fund commitments.

title: US-Iran Deal Stirs Inflation Fears After China Kept Oil in Check
summary: The US-Iran deal is setting the stage for a recovery in Chinese oil demand that risks driving up global inflation pressures, according to Bloomberg Economics, assuming the agreement holds and eventually restores energy flows to the world’s second-biggest economy.

title: Deutsche Bank Favors US Over Euro Corporate Bonds on Iran Impact
summary: European corporate bonds are vulnerable to the aftershocks of the war in Iran, with spreads for both investment grade and junk bonds expected to widen by year end, according to strategists at Deutsche Bank AG.

title: Gold Jumps After US and Iran Agree Deal That Would Reopen Hormuz
summary: Gold and silver rallied after the US and Iran announced an interim deal to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing global inflation fears and potentially tempering expectations for interest-rate hikes.

title: Iran War’s ‘Inflationary Legacy’ Key to Stock Rally’s Longevity
summary: Inflation worries kept investors cautious even as bonds and stocks rallied on Monday after a US-Iran interim deal eased nerves, with market participants warning that the conflict’s economic fallout remains unresolved.
